_comment: Cosmos DB audit container immutability policy.
This file documents the required immutable container configuration for the 'audit' container.
The 'audit' container stores ADR-015 Tier 2 compliance records for AI interactions.
Immutability: container-level policy prevents updates and deletes at the data plane.
IMPORTANT: The application code in AuditLogService.cs enforces append-only semantics
by using CreateItemAsync exclusively. This infrastructure policy provides a second,
independent enforcement layer that protects audit records even if application code
is modified or a data contributor role is used directly.
To apply this policy, use the Azure Portal or Azure CLI (see commands below).
Note: Cosmos DB point-in-time restore immutability policies are currently preview.

immutabilityPolicy._comment: Cosmos DB immutability policy (Time-Based Retention). Set at provisioning time.

rbac._comment: Only Cosmos DB Built-in Data Reader role should be assigned for audit reads.
rbac.writeRole: Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor (restricted to CreateItemAsync by application code)
rbac.noDeleteRole: DeleteItemAsync and ReplaceItemAsync are blocked by immutability policy

deploymentCommands._comment: CLI commands to verify immutability policy after deployment.
deploymentCommands.verifyPolicy: az cosmosdb sql container show --account-name spaarke-cosmos-dev --database-name spaarke-ai --name audit --resource-group spe-infrastructure-westus2 --query 'resource.defaultTtl'
deploymentCommands.applyImmutabilityViaBicep: See infrastructure/bicep/modules/cosmos-db.bicep — add immutabilityPolicy to auditContainer resource block when GA.
